text: A grainy surveillance still was run through facial recognition and returned Robert Williams as a candidate. Police allegedly relied on the output and a flawed photo identification process, leading to a wrongful arrest for a theft he did not commit.
ai_system_name: Michigan State Police / DataWorks Plus facial-recognition pipeline
deployer: Detroit Police Department and Michigan State Police
canonical_source_conflicted: Actual suspect identity and warrant probable-cause standards
mitigation_gap: No policy requiring independent corroboration before arrest; warrant materials did not adequately disclose the limits of the face-recognition lead.
reliance_or_harm: Williams was arrested, detained, and later brought a civil-rights suit; settlement included damages and policy reforms.
